concept.conceptName: Triacylglycerol lipase | Serum or Plasma | Chemistry - non-challenge

en.name: Lipase in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Pancreatic lipase is a digestive enzyme secreted by the exocrine pancreas that hydrolyses dietary triglycerides. Serum lipase activity ≥ 3 × the upper limit of normal is one of the three diagnostic criteria for acute pancreatitis under the revised Atlanta classification [1] and the IAP/APA guidelines [2]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a colorimetric or turbidimetric assay. Results are reported as an enzyme-activity concentration in `U/L` (international units per litre) or, in SI, in `µkat/L` [3]. ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on one LOINC Hierarchy concept (scoped to `Chemistry - non-challenge`), expanded to include all its descendants. The set resolves to one LOINC "Lab Test" concept: - *3004905 / 3040-3 — Lipase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)* **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`[iU]/L`** (concept 8923 — international unit per litre, written `U/L` in clinical reports). SI conversion: `1 U/L = 0.01667 µkat/L` (derived from the katal SI definition, see NPU03612 vs NPU57165 [3]). Reference ranges are highly method-dependent (no international standard for lipase activity measurement); preserve the upper limit of normal from the source lab. ## Excluded Concepts The following LOINC concept in Serum or Plasma is deliberately excluded by name: - *1761825 / 100743-4 — Lipase.macromolecular [Presence] in Serum or Plasma*: qualitative test for macrolipasaemia, not a routine activity measurement. In addition, the set deliberately omits: - **Lipase in urine, amniotic fluid, body fluid, peritoneal fluid, pleural fluid, dialysis fluid specimens**: out of scope for a "blood" concept set. - **Sincalide challenge codes (`--Nth specimen post Sincalide`)**: challenge tests, out of scope. - **Cord blood and fetus specimens**: physiologically distinct from post-natal blood. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports a lipase value: `3004905 / 3040-3 — Lipase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma`. - **Source reports lipase from ascitic fluid, peritoneal fluid, pleural fluid**: do **not** map. Out of scope. - **Do not confuse with**: amylase (separate concept set, different enzyme), lipoprotein lipase (different enzyme, lipid metabolism). ## References [1] Banks PA, Bollen TL, Dervenis C, Gooszen HG, Johnson CD, Sarr MG, et al. Classification of acute pancreatitis—2012: revision of the Atlanta classification and definitions by international consensus. *Gut*. 2013;62(1):102-11. Available: https://doi.org/10.1136/gutjnl-2012-302779 [2] Working Group IAP/APA Acute Pancreatitis Guidelines. IAP/APA evidence-based guidelines for the management of acute pancreatitis. *Pancreatology*. 2013;13(4 Suppl 2):e1-15. Available: https://doi.org/10.1016/j.pan.2013.07.063 [3] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
